ANON DATA
Marketing Dossier / 90-Day Operating Plan / Demonstration — anonymized

AI Drafting Assistant
for small law firms

Not “another AI chat” but an assistant that drafts a lawsuit in minutes — for a lawyer without an in-house assistant. Win the “document drafting” niche in 90 days through one warm channel.

SUBJECT  LegalTech SaaS SEGMENT  Firms of 2–10, no assistant HORIZON  4 sprints · 90 days STATUS  Pre-launch hypotheses

01

Partnership with legal CRMs — a warm channel straight to buyers.

02

Offer with money-back guarantee — removes the “it won’t work” fear.

03

02

Priorities & the 90-day map

RICE · roadmap · channels

RICE landscape

Bubble: x = effort, y = impact, size = reach. Top RICE bet highlighted in gold. Straight from tasks.

RICE score ranking

Bar = RICE score per task, highest first.

90-day roadmap

Stacked bars = offset + duration per sprint (Sprint 0 → 3). Roadmap shape is an estimate derived from the strategy map.

Channel mix

Priority weighting across acquisition channels — estimate (paid traffic deferred).

Acquisition funnel

Illustrative funnel anchored on the north-star (30 paying firms/mo). Upstream stages are estimates.

Effort vs. reach by lever

Radar of total reach and ease (inverse effort) per dimension — from tasks.

03

Top tasks — RICE backlog

sorted by score
#TaskLever RICERICE
04

Full strategy

expand to read
▶ Strategy (demo) — positioning, segment, offer, channels, 90-day map

1. Positioning

For small-firm lawyers drowning in routine drafts — an AI assistant that produces a first draft in minutes. We compete not with large LegalTech platforms but with “doing it by hand” and “plain ChatGPT”.

2. Focus segment

Law firms of 2–10 people with no in-house assistant: acute pain, fast decision cycle.

3. Offer

A subscription with a first-month money-back guarantee removes the “it won’t work” fear.

4. Channels

Priority — partnerships with legal CRMs and niche communities; paid traffic deferred.

5. 90-day map

S0 — analytics and offer page  ·  S1 — partner channel  ·  S2 — content  ·  S3 — scale.

05

Red-team & fact-check

adversarial review
Strongest counter-case

Trust, not traffic, is the bottleneck

“The bottleneck is not marketing but trust in draft quality: if a lawyer gets one bad draft, they won’t come back. Quality first, then acquisition.”

Riskiest assumptions
  • Lawyers will trust AI with drafts
  • Partner CRMs will open their base
  • The guarantee won’t attract freeloaders
Claims check

Hypotheses flagged — no fabricated numbers

✓Demo: hypotheses are flagged, no fabricated numbers.
“target 30 firms/month”
Issue: no baseline.
Fix: flag as hypothesis until first sales.
“LegalTech market is growing”
Source example.com · confidence: medium
Open question: real willingness to pay?
› ai-seo › pricing › copywriting › ajtbd › marketing-psychology
METHODOLOGY · Fan-out across ~9 marketing perspectives on a shared fact pack (Ground) → red-team → synthesis → RICE backlog → double fact-check → executive one-pager. RICE charts are computed strictly from the tasks array; roadmap, channel mix and funnel are estimates derived from the strategy map and are labeled as such.
DEMO REPORT · Generated by the Render phase on anonymized data · Charts: Chart.js · Not financial or legal advice.
